title: Minor Characters (Rebuild)
wikitext:
{{expand}} ''Organization and layout to be worked on as we go.'' Eventually should include seiyuu, episodes featured, and picture for everyone (where applicable). =Supporting Cast= ==Wunder Bridge Crew== The members of the [[AAA Wunder|AAA Wunder's]] bridge crew introduced in [[Evangelion 3.0]]. All are members of [[Wille]]. {{MontageBox |float=right |columns=2 |image1=[[Image:Eva3-33_C0521_b_Takao.jpg|200px]] |image2=[[Image:Eva3-33_C0225_Kitakami.jpg|200px]] |image3=[[Image:Eva3-33_C0456C_Nagara.jpg|200px]] |image4=[[Image:Eva3-33_C0441_Tama.jpg|200px]] |caption=
* Takao * Nagara * Kitakami * Tama
}} ===Koji Takao === *Appearance: [[Evangelion 3.0]]; [[Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0 Thrice Upon a Time]] *''Seiyu: Akio Otsuka'' *Notes: Appears to be the ships engineer. Controls the engine ignition sequence with Tama. Seems to know or be a friend of [[Ryoji Kaji (Rebuild)|Kaji]]. Named after the heavy cruiser [[Warships_in_Evangelion#IJN_Takao.28.E9.AB.98.E9.9B.84.29|Takao]]. The choice of Akio Otsuka as his ''seiyu'' appears to have been influenced by Otsuka's previous role as Captain Nemo in Hideaki Anno's older series ''Nadia: The Secret of Blue Water''. Takao's lines during Wunder's startup sequence, in particular, are directly drawn from Captain Nemo's lines during the New Natilus's startup sequence. ===Midori Kitakami === *Appearance: [[Evangelion 3.0]]; [[Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0 Thrice Upon a Time]] *''Seiyu: Mariya Ise'' *Notes: Appears to be assigned to sonar and radar. An inexperienced crew member. Her station is covered with sticky notes left by someone else. Named after the "torpedo cruiser" [[Warships_in_Evangelion#IJN_Kitakami.28.E5.8C.97.E4.B8.8A.29|Kitakami]]. Her character design appears to pay homage to Gay Ellis from the old science-fiction series UFO.http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/14996/Kitakami-Midori-new-original-character-or-mimic/ She is also the star of the [[Evangelion: 3.0 (-46h)]] OVA. ===Sumire Nagara === *Appearance: [[Evangelion 3.0]]; [[Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0 Thrice Upon a Time]] *''Seiyu: Sayaka Ohara (Also participated in 1.0 and 2.0 as a Nerv operator.)'' *Notes: The Wunder's helmsman. Controls the Wunder and the fleet in flight. Never flew before prior to the Wunder's launch. Named after the light cruiser [[Warships_in_Evangelion#IJN_Nagara.28.E9.95.B7.E8.89.AF.29|Nagara]] and possibly also the Matsu Flight II/Tachibana class destroyer Sumire. ===Hideki Tama=== *Appearance: [[Evangelion 3.0]]; [[Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0 Thrice Upon a Time]] *''Seiyu: Anri Katsu'' *Notes: Appears to be assigned to engineering. An inexperienced crew member. His station is packed with technical manuals. Named after the light cruiser [[Warships_in_Evangelion#IJN_Tama.28.E5.A4.9A.E6.91.A9.29|Tama]]. ==Village-3 residents== Residents of Village-3 seen in [[Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0 Thrice Upon a Time]]. ===Village Ladies=== A group of middle-aged to elderly women who help [[Rei Q]] get adjusted to life in the village, teaching her about human society, farming, and other things. ===Village Lady A=== ===Village Lady B=== ===Village Lady C=== ===Village Lady D=== ===Village Little Girl=== *Appearance: [[Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0]] *''Seiyu:???'' *Notes: A little girl who gifts Rei Q a radish after her first day of work. Later, she also gives Rei Q a book after they meet again in the library-wagon. ===Bunzaemon Horaki=== *Appearance: [[Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0]] *''Seiyu:???'' *Notes: [[Hikari Horaki]]'s father, grumpy at Shinji's apparent disrespect in not eating. Probably gets his given name from Chiltonian's full name in the ''Ultra Seven''-inspired parody manga Prefectural Earth Defense Force. ===Tsubame Suzuhara=== *Appearance: [[Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0]] *''Seiyu:???'' *Notes: Toji and Hikari's baby, very likely named after the Tsubame service on the Kyushu Shinkansen, preserving the Shinkansen theme naming in Hikari's family. (Tsubame's a boy, right? Not 100 percent sure.) ===Hajime Aida=== *Appearance: [[Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0]] (Grave marker only) *''Seiyu: (none)'' *Notes: Is not seen onscreen, having died in the events following Near-Third Impact, with Kensuke not knowing him well enough prior to his passing. A visit to his grave provides context for Kensuke to tell Shinji to try to talk to his father before it is too late. =Others= Whoever doesn't belong above but doesn't belong below. =Mentions= We hear about them, but we neither see them nor hear them. (At least, not as far as we know.) Some are of no significance whatsoever, but are included for reasons of pedantry. {{reflist}} {{Character List}} [[Category:Characters in Rebuild of Evangelion]] [[Category:A to Z]]
html:
"Making something... Nurturing something is really great. You can see and learn so many things from the process."
Please help improve this article or section by expanding it.
If you have questions on what to improve/expand, please discuss on the talk page.

Organization and layout to be worked on as we go.

Eventually should include seiyuu, episodes featured, and picture for everyone (where applicable).

Contents

  • 1 Supporting Cast
    • 1.1 Wunder Bridge Crew
      • 1.1.1 Koji Takao
      • 1.1.2 Midori Kitakami
      • 1.1.3 Sumire Nagara
      • 1.1.4 Hideki Tama
    • 1.2 Village-3 residents
      • 1.2.1 Village Ladies
      • 1.2.2 Village Lady A
      • 1.2.3 Village Lady B
      • 1.2.4 Village Lady C
      • 1.2.5 Village Lady D
      • 1.2.6 Village Little Girl
      • 1.2.7 Bunzaemon Horaki
      • 1.2.8 Tsubame Suzuhara
      • 1.2.9 Hajime Aida
  • 2 Others
  • 3 Mentions

Supporting Cast[edit]

Wunder Bridge Crew[edit]

The members of the AAA Wunder's bridge crew introduced in Evangelion 3.0. All are members of Wille.

  • Takao
  • Nagara
  • Kitakami
  • Tama

Koji Takao[edit]

  • Appearance: Evangelion 3.0; Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0 Thrice Upon a Time
  • Seiyu: Akio Otsuka
  • Notes: Appears to be the ships engineer. Controls the engine ignition sequence with Tama. Seems to know or be a friend of Kaji. Named after the heavy cruiser Takao. The choice of Akio Otsuka as his seiyu appears to have been influenced by Otsuka's previous role as Captain Nemo in Hideaki Anno's older series Nadia: The Secret of Blue Water. Takao's lines during Wunder's startup sequence, in particular, are directly drawn from Captain Nemo's lines during the New Natilus's startup sequence.

Midori Kitakami[edit]

  • Appearance: Evangelion 3.0; Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0 Thrice Upon a Time
  • Seiyu: Mariya Ise
  • Notes: Appears to be assigned to sonar and radar. An inexperienced crew member. Her station is covered with sticky notes left by someone else. Named after the "torpedo cruiser" Kitakami. Her character design appears to pay homage to Gay Ellis from the old science-fiction series UFO.[1] She is also the star of the Evangelion: 3.0 (-46h) OVA.

Sumire Nagara[edit]

  • Appearance: Evangelion 3.0; Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0 Thrice Upon a Time
  • Seiyu: Sayaka Ohara (Also participated in 1.0 and 2.0 as a Nerv operator.)
  • Notes: The Wunder's helmsman. Controls the Wunder and the fleet in flight. Never flew before prior to the Wunder's launch. Named after the light cruiser Nagara and possibly also the Matsu Flight II/Tachibana class destroyer Sumire.

Hideki Tama[edit]

  • Appearance: Evangelion 3.0; Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0 Thrice Upon a Time
  • Seiyu: Anri Katsu
  • Notes: Appears to be assigned to engineering. An inexperienced crew member. His station is packed with technical manuals. Named after the light cruiser Tama.

Village-3 residents[edit]

Residents of Village-3 seen in Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0 Thrice Upon a Time.

Village Ladies[edit]

A group of middle-aged to elderly women who help Rei Q get adjusted to life in the village, teaching her about human society, farming, and other things.

Village Lady A[edit]

Village Lady B[edit]

Village Lady C[edit]

Village Lady D[edit]

Village Little Girl[edit]

  • Appearance: Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0
  • Seiyu:???
  • Notes: A little girl who gifts Rei Q a radish after her first day of work. Later, she also gives Rei Q a book after they meet again in the library-wagon.

Bunzaemon Horaki[edit]

  • Appearance: Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0
  • Seiyu:???
  • Notes: Hikari Horaki's father, grumpy at Shinji's apparent disrespect in not eating. Probably gets his given name from Chiltonian's full name in the Ultra Seven-inspired parody manga Prefectural Earth Defense Force.

Tsubame Suzuhara[edit]

  • Appearance: Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0
  • Seiyu:???
  • Notes: Toji and Hikari's baby, very likely named after the Tsubame service on the Kyushu Shinkansen, preserving the Shinkansen theme naming in Hikari's family. (Tsubame's a boy, right? Not 100 percent sure.)

Hajime Aida[edit]

  • Appearance: Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0 (Grave marker only)
  • Seiyu: (none)
  • Notes: Is not seen onscreen, having died in the events following Near-Third Impact, with Kensuke not knowing him well enough prior to his passing. A visit to his grave provides context for Kensuke to tell Shinji to try to talk to his father before it is too late.

Others[edit]

Whoever doesn't belong above but doesn't belong below.

Mentions[edit]

We hear about them, but we neither see them nor hear them. (At least, not as far as we know.) Some are of no significance whatsoever, but are included for reasons of pedantry.

  1. ↑ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/14996/Kitakami-Midori-new-original-character-or-mimic/
Characters
Main characters: Shinji Ikari | Rei Ayanami | Asuka Langley Soryu
Nerv staff: Misato Katsuragi | Gendo Ikari | Ritsuko Akagi | Kozo Fuyutsuki | Ryoji Kaji | Maya Ibuki | Makoto Hyuga | Shigeru Aoba
Classmates: Toji Suzuhara | Kensuke Aida | Hikari Horaki
Other characters: Kaworu Nagisa | Yui Ikari | Naoko Akagi | Kyoko Zeppelin Soryu | Dr. Katsuragi | Mr. Langley | Keel Lorenz | Pen Pen
Rebuild of Evangelion: Asuka Shikinami Langley | Mari Makinami Illustrious | Sakura Suzuhara | Ryoji Kaji (Jr)
